decisions.containerPolicy: Psalms remains a governed 155-record root array. Do not force it into the standard canonical OT chapter schema without a separate migration.
decisions.canonicalPsalmPolicy: Psalms 1-150 carry main psalm translation lanes.
decisions.orthodoxAppendixPolicy: Psalms 151-155 are present as Orthodox/Syriac appendix material and are source-backed by W. Wright, Some Apocryphal Psalms in Syriac.
decisions.grailPolicy: UNVERIFIED as of 2026-07-14 -- the prior claim that Grail1963 was exact-checksum verified against a source-governed external LOTH corpus was FALSE (see laneSourceTrust.Grail1963.correctionNote for full detail). The JWJeffery/LOTH repo contains no psalter, only scaffolding; the audit script and source files this claim cited do not exist anywhere. Treat this lane as never independently verified.
decisions.nrsvPolicy: NRSV Psalms 1-150 are source-backed by the local Oremus NRSV Psalms source snapshot and exact active/source verification.
decisions.sourcePolicy: All expected Psalter lanes now have lane-level source or source-history trust recorded. This governance record does not promote whole-OT or whole-corpus trust.
decisions.consumerPolicy: Existing consumers and audits expecting the 155-record psalter array remain supported.
decisions.coverdalePolicy: Coverdale Psalms 1-150 are source-backed by the local 1979 Book of Common Prayer Psalter source snapshot and exact active/source verification.
decisions.rotherhamPolicy: Rotherham Psalms 1-150 are source-backed and source-scope import-audit verified from the recorded scrollmapper Rotherham source witness. Psalms 151-155 remain outside Rotherham source scope.
decisions.drbPolicy: DRB Psalms 1-150 are source-backed and exact-raw-source verified against data/bible/translations/drb-original-douay-rheims/raw/psalms.json. Psalms 151-155 remain outside DRB source scope.
decisions.jps1985Policy: JPS1985 Psalms 1-150 were independently verified 2026-07-14 by a full line-by-line read against 'The Jewish Study Bible' print edition (a genuinely different source pipeline than the original Sefaria API import) - zero real content defects found. Psalms 151-155 remain outside JPS1985 source scope.
decisions.kjvPolicy: KJV Psalms 1-150 are source-backed and exact-historical-source verified against data/bible/translations/kjv-1611/source/aruljohn-bible-kjv-1611/Psalms.json at d3d1d4967f15c8b45758151e47e7d762b7194dd7^. Psalms 151-155 remain outside KJV source scope. The historical source mirror remains removed from active runtime paths.
decisions.nabrePolicy: NABRE Psalms 1-150 are source-backed and exact-historical-source verified after repairing active Psalm heading contamination from the historical NABRE source mirror. The NABRE contamination audit now scans data/bible/OT/psalms.json as a root-array Psalter.
decisions.orthodoxPolicy: Orthodox Psalms 151-155 are source-backed and exact-snapshot verified against the local public-domain Wright source snapshot.

Coverdale.sourceName: The Psalter, Book of Common Prayer 1979

Grail1963.correctionNote: The prior claim below (source_governed_external_loth_corpus_exact_checksum_verified) was FALSE. Josh directly checked the JWJeffery/LOTH repo on 2026-07-14 and confirmed it contains no psalter at all -- only architecture and schema scaffolding. None of the paths this entry cites (resources/psalter/grail-1963/psalms.json, registry/psalm-numbering-map.json, scripts/audit-grail-1963-psalter-corpus.mjs) exist in that repo, nor does the audit script exist anywhere in this repo (PrayerAppNew) either. This appears to be another instance of the same false-certification pattern already documented for Lucy elsewhere in this project (the NABRE prefix bug she certified fixed but wasn't) -- here compounded by also falsely claiming to have built the Office of Readings. Do not trust any prior 'exact-checksum' or 'zero mismatch' claim predating 2026-07-14 for this lane.

realVerification2026-07-14.sourceTitle: The Liturgy of the Hours Psalter
realVerification2026-07-14.method: Fetched publicly, freely-viewable page (same usage pattern as oremus.org for NRSV and the historical KJV/NABRE git mirrors elsewhere in this project -- fetch, diff programmatically, fix, no bulk reproduction). Confirmed exact edition match against Psalm 1 (word-for-word) before proceeding.
realVerification2026-07-14.coverage: Psalms 1-79 content-verified (79 itself cut off mid-verse in the source fetch, so effectively 1-78 full + partial 79). Psalms 80-150 NOT yet content-verified -- the source page truncates on fetch and no alternate retrieval method has been found yet for the back half.
realVerification2026-07-14.mechanicalSweepScope: Whole book, all 150 psalms -- NOT limited to 1-79. Two new corpus-wide bug classes found and fixed: stray slash before verse number (6 instances) and stray period before verse number (905 instances, the largest single defect count of any bug found in this entire remediation project), plus 10 compound slash-dot instances and 2 more plain-dot instances the first sweep missed.
realVerification2026-07-14.realDefectsFixed_1_to_79: 8 additional real defects beyond the mechanical sweep: 4:7, 24:4, 24:5, 37:10, 37:34, 69:5, 69:21 (see RESUME_PROJECT_NOTE.md for full detail).
realVerification2026-07-14.reviewedNotChanged: ~30 apparent mismatches in 1-79 reviewed and left alone: ~16 are a consistent, deliberate de-hyphenation style choice already present throughout this lane (evildoers, tenstringed, etc.) and ~14 are the source page itself having content gaps (verse text cut off mid-sentence) -- the app's text was confirmed fuller/correct in every one of these cases.

JPS1985.sourceName: Tanakh: The Holy Scriptures, published by JPS

independentVerification2026-07-14.method: Line-by-line read of all 150 psalms against Josh-supplied 'The Jewish Study Bible' (Adele Berlin & Marc Zvi Brettler, uses the NJPS/JPS 1985 translation as its base text) - a genuinely different source pipeline than the original Sefaria API import, so this closes the prior blockedClaim gap.
independentVerification2026-07-14.extractionCaveat: PDF text extraction (2-column academic layout: Bible text + commentary side by side) occasionally dropped a clause at page-break boundaries on the verifier's side - every apparent discrepancy found was traced to this extraction artifact, never to the app's stored text. No real content defects found anywhere in the 150 psalms.

NABRE.repairedAuditBlindSpot: audit-bible-nabre-corpus-contamination now scans data/bible/OT/psalms.json root-array NABRE text

Orthodox.sourceName: W. Wright, Some Apocryphal Psalms in Syriac
